name: Bolivia
avoid: January–March rains can flood sections of Salar de Uyuni, muddy desert tracks, and cause landslides on long road journeys.
Altitude is immediate in La Paz, Copacabana and Potosí, so keep the first 24 hours light and hydrate before adding Cerro Rico or Tiwanaku day trips.
Road blockades and strike-related transport disruptions are not rare in Bolivia; keep buffers before domestic flights and international connections.

cities.name: La Paz
cities.notes: High-altitude capital for cable car views, Witches' Market lanes, lunar badlands at Valle de la Luna and Tiwanaku day trips.

cities.name: Uyuni
cities.notes: Gateway to Salar de Uyuni for the train cemetery, Colchani salt processing, cactus islands and mirror-season photography.

cities.name: Sucre
cities.notes: Whitewashed constitutional capital with convent viewpoints, Bolivian independence history and a slower pace than La Paz.

cities.name: Copacabana
cities.notes: Lake Titicaca base for the basilica, Cerro Calvario sunsets and day boats to Isla del Sol.

cities.name: Potosí
cities.notes: UNESCO silver-boom city for Casa Nacional de la Moneda, colonial churches and Cerro Rico mine history.

itinerary.sim: Entel or Tigo prepaid SIMs are the easiest pickup in La Paz; expect roughly ₹720–₹1,440 for 5–10GB. Download offline maps before Uyuni and Copacabana because coverage drops outside town and across the salt flats.

La Paz.name: La Paz
La Paz.note: Sleep centrally in Sopocachi or near the cable cars, keep day one lighter for altitude, and slot Tiwanaku on a separate early start if you want the archaeology without rushing.

days.theme: Historic Center, Cable Cars & Valle de la Luna

activities.name: Plaza Murillo, San Francisco Basilica exterior and Witches' Market walk
Mi Teleférico ride across central La Paz and El Alto viewpoints
Valle de la Luna entrance
Moon Valley taxi or shared transfer from Sopocachi / Centro
Tiwanaku archaeological complex and museums entry
Shared Tiwanaku day tour or return transport from La Paz
Calle Jaén museums or a slow café stop back in La Paz

Copacabana.name: Copacabana
Copacabana.note: Copacabana is best as a slower lake break between big transit legs, and it also works well if you plan to continue onward to Peru after Bolivia.

activities.name: Basilica of Our Lady of Copacabana and town square
Cerro Calvario sunset walk above the bay
Pedalo or short boat ride on the Copacabana waterfront
Round-trip public boat to Isla del Sol
Isla del Sol community / trail fee
Pilkokaina ruins and Yumani stairways walk

Sucre.name: Sucre
Sucre.note: Sucre is gentler for acclimatization than La Paz or Potosí, so it works well as a reset city for longer café mornings and museum-heavy afternoons.

activities.name: Plaza 25 de Mayo, Metropolitan Cathedral exterior and Casa de la Libertad
La Recoleta viewpoint and monastery area
ASUR Indigenous Art Museum

days.theme: Market Morning & Slow White City Afternoon

activities.name: Mercado Central breakfast and salteñas
Cal Orck'o dinosaur park or Museo del Tesoro
Sunset drinks on a rooftop terrace in the old town

Potosí.name: Potosí
Potosí.note: Potosí is colder and higher than Sucre, so layer up for morning mine tours and book a reputable operator that includes proper helmets, boots and masks for Cerro Rico.

days.theme: Casa de la Moneda & Colonial Potosí

activities.name: Casa Nacional de la Moneda guided entry
Plaza 10 de Noviembre and San Lorenzo church façade walk
Santa Teresa Convent Museum or Torre de la Compañía viewpoint
Cerro Rico guided mine tour with gear and miner gifts
Mineral market stop before entering the mine
Warm café recovery back in the center after the tour

Uyuni.name: Uyuni
Uyuni.note: Lock your Uyuni tour operator, vehicle condition and English-guide needs before arrival, and pack sun protection because the salt glare is intense even in cool dry-season weather.

days.theme: Train Cemetery & Salar de Uyuni Classic Circuit

activities.name: Shared full-day 4x4 Salar de Uyuni tour covering Train Cemetery, Colchani, salt flats and sunset
Incahuasi Island entry during the classic circuit
Perspective-photo stop and Dakar monument
Shared 3-day Salar de Uyuni and southwest lagoons tour with basic lodging
Eduardo Avaroa reserve and laguna fees usually paid separately
Upgrade to a private room or English-speaking guide if you do not want the standard shared setup

meals: The Hot Spot The New Religion

connections.method: Bus or shared taxi via the Tiquina crossing; the direct ride is usually 3.5–4 hrs and easy to arrange the day before
Return to La Paz by bus or shared taxi, then continue on a BOA or Amaszonas domestic flight to Sucre if you want to save a full transit day; overnight buses are the cheaper fallback
Frequent Flota bus services and shared taxis connect Sucre to Potosí in roughly 3 hrs
Flota bus or shared taxi over the Altiplano; allow 4–5 hrs and keep warm layers handy for early departures

extras: Dry season from May to October gives the simplest logistics for Uyuni, Potosí and road transfers, while January to March is better only if you specifically want mirror-effect photos and can tolerate weather disruption.
Carry cash in bolivianos for museum tickets, mine tours, island fees and rural food stops because card acceptance is patchy outside better hotels in La Paz and Sucre.
Bolivia rewards slow acclimatization: start in La Paz with lighter sightseeing, then do Tiwanaku, Cerro Rico and long overland hops after you feel settled.
If you only have a week, keep La Paz, Uyuni and either Sucre or Copacabana as your core; add both Potosí and Copacabana only when you have the extra transit time.
For onward travel, Copacabana links naturally with Peru's Lake Titicaca side, while Uyuni fits well with overland connections into Chile's Atacama region.

La Paz: Valle de la Luna La Paz
Copacabana: Basilica of Our Lady of Copacabana Bolivia
Potosí: Casa Nacional de la Moneda Potosi
